features.description: library-lint CLI: structural validation of SKILL.md files (frontmatter, line count, no project-specific refs, no hardcoded paths, referenced files exist)
features.acceptance: library-lint runs on all skills, reports violations with file:line, exits 0 when clean, exits 1 when violations found. Catches: missing name/description/version, >500 lines, cowork/pete-pa refs, ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}, absolute paths outside ~/RepoBase/, broken relative file references.

features.description: library-lint: tool independence check — verify no cross-imports between tool packages under tools/
features.acceptance: library-lint --tools scans all .py files under tools/*/ and reports any imports from sibling tool packages. tools/library_management/ importing from tools/some_other/ is a violation. Importing from stdlib, third-party packages, or within the same package is fine.

features.description: library-health CLI: scan ~/RepoBase/ for consumer projects and report ecosystem health
features.acceptance: library-health discovers consumer projects by scanning ~/RepoBase/*/.claude/settings.json for additionalDirectories pointing to shared-skills. For each consumer: checks symlinks are valid, reports broken/missing/stale symlinks, reports if library-link is needed. Also reports uncommitted changes in shared-skills itself.

features.description: Git pre-commit hook: runs library-lint on staged SKILL.md and .py files, blocks commit on violations
features.acceptance: scripts/pre-commit exists as a git hook script. Only lints files that are staged (git diff --cached). Exits 0 if clean, exits 1 with violation report if not. install-hooks command or library-setup installs the hook to .git/hooks/pre-commit.

features.description: SessionStart hook: runs library-health on session start in shared-skills repo
features.acceptance: shared-skills .claude/settings.json has a SessionStart hook running library-health. Reports consumer project status and any ecosystem issues at session start. Non-blocking (exit 0 always).

features.description: Register library-lint and library-health in pyproject.toml, update library-management SKILL.md with new tools
features.acceptance: pyproject.toml has library-lint and library-health entries. library-management SKILL.md documents both tools with usage examples. poetry install makes both available.
